title: How to Buy Golem (GLM) — Step-by-Step Guide
content:
Golem is a decentralized computing marketplace that connects resource providers with those who need processing power for intensive tasks, including AI development. Because of its focus on distributed infrastructure, it is a key player in the AI-focused crypto sector. | Metric | Details | |

## Getting Started with Golem Golem (GLM) is available on major cryptocurrency exchanges, including Binance, Coinbase, and Bybit. The process to acquire it involves several standard steps: 1. Create an account on a reputable exchange that supports GLM trading pairs. 2. Complete the mandatory Identity Verification (KYC) processes required by the platform. 3. Deposit fiat currency or transfer crypto assets to your exchange wallet. 4. Navigate to the trading section and locate the GLM pair (e.g., GLM/USDT). 5. Place a market order for immediate execution or a limit order to specify your desired entry price.

## Storage and Safety After purchasing your tokens, it is generally recommended to move them to a secure, self-custodial wallet if you do not plan to trade them actively. Hardware wallets offer the highest level of security for long-term storage.

## Strategic Considerations Before deciding to purchase, consider the following:
- Risk Score: At 8/10, Golem is a high-risk asset. Only consider allocation as part of a balanced portfolio.
- Market Volatility: Golem has experienced a 24-hour change of -0.24% and significant price fluctuations over the last year.
- Competition: Golem competes with other infrastructure tokens like [Render](/render-token) and [BitTorrent](/bittorrent); research how they differ.

## Tax Implications Tax regulations regarding cryptocurrency vary by jurisdiction. In many regions, selling or trading tokens is a taxable event. Consult with a qualified professional to understand the reporting requirements for your location.
